description: Synchrony Financial is a U.S. consumer finance company that issues store cards, co-branded cards, installment loans, health financing products, and online banking deposits through Synchrony Bank.

overview.title: Business model
paragraphs: Synchrony sits behind many branded checkout-credit offers, providing private-label, dual, co-branded, and general-purpose credit cards along with short- and long-term installment lending. The company organizes its lending around five commercial platforms: Home & Auto, Digital, Diversified & Value, Health & Wellness, and Lifestyle.

Its model depends on merchant and provider partnerships: retailers get financing tools and customer-loyalty machinery, while Synchrony owns the accounts and receivables. This is the quiet financial plumbing of American commerce, with a rewards brochure on top and underwriting underneath.

overview.title: Products and footprint
paragraphs: The repo seed products map cleanly to Synchrony's public positioning. Synchrony credit cards cover retail, marketplace, network, and partner-branded programs, while CareCredit is the company's health and wellness financing brand for medical, dental, vision, veterinary, cosmetic, and related care.

Synchrony Bank also funds the lending business through consumer deposits, including high-yield savings, money market accounts, CDs, and IRA products. In 2025, Synchrony reported $182.3 billion of purchase volume, $103.8 billion of loan receivables, and 70.7 million active accounts.

overview.title: Registry angle
paragraphs: For Free The World, Synchrony is less a consumer app company than an embedded-credit infrastructure company. The relevant comparison set should include credit unions, public-benefit banking, transparent point-of-sale financing, lower-cost health payment models, open banking portability, and consumer-controlled financial data tools.

The strongest critical lens is CareCredit and deferred-interest medical financing, where the advertised convenience can become expensive if terms are poorly understood or balances are not paid within the promotional period. The product is useful for access, but the fine print does a great deal of the heavy lifting.
